title: exallage radicans
intro: Exallage radicans, also known as poison ivy, is a perennial plant with glossy leaves that contain a toxic sap called urushiol, causing severe skin irritation and allergic reactions in many people. Traditionally, the roots of poison ivy have been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and acting as an antiseptic, making it useful in treating wounds, burns, and skin infections. Herbal preparations of poison ivy, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, can be made by drying and processing the roots, but caution is advised due to the plant's potent and potentially irritating properties.
